content.problemStatement:
There are NNN students in a class, where the iii-th student has a score of AiA_iAi​.

The iii-th student will boast if and only if the number of students scoring less than or equal AiA_iAi​ is greater than the number of students scoring greater than AiA_iAi​.

Find the number of students who will boast.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the number of students.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ - the scores of the students.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the number of students who will boast.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of students. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN, the scores of the students.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of students who will boast.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: All scores are equal, so every student has cntLe = 3 > 0 and boasts.
Test case 2: Sorted scores are [1,2,3]; threshold = a[1] = 2. Scores >=2 are 2 and 3, so 2 students boast.
Test case 3: Sorted scores are [1,30,30,30]; threshold = a[2] = 30. All three students with score 30 boast.
Sorted array: [1,2,2,3,4]; threshold = a[2] = 2. Elements >=2 are 2,2,3,4 → 4 students boast.

solution.approach: For a student with score Ai, let cntLe be the number of scores <= Ai. The student boasts iff cntLe > N - cntLe, i.e., cntLe > N/2. After sorting the scores, cntLe for the element at index i (0‑based) equals i+1. The smallest score that satisfies the condition is the element at index floor(N/2). Let threshold = sorted[floor(N/2)]. Every student whose score is >= threshold will have cntLe > N/2, because all those scores are counted in cntLe. Therefore the answer is the number of elements >= threshold, which can be obtained by a binary search (lower_bound) or a linear scan after sorting. Complexity per test case: O(N log N) time for sorting, O(N) additional for counting, O(N) extra space for the array.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def solve():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = next(it)
        a = [next(it) for _ in range(N)]
        a.sort()
        threshold = a[N//2]
        # count elements >= threshold
        cnt = N - next((i for i, v in enumerate(a) if v >= threshold), N)
        out_lines.append(str(cnt))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
